titleInfo.title: Chūgoku ni okeru hokkekyō no sekkoku
中国における法華經の石刻
中國境内的法華經石刻
Stone Inscriptions of the ‘Lotus Sutra’ in China

mods.abstract: Carving Buddhist sutras on stone in China could be understood as a combination of the tradition of carving Confucian Classics on stone and the Buddhist practice of copying and printing the Buddhist sutras. Lotus Sutra, especially the Chapter of the Universal Gate of the Bodhisattva Avalokiteśvara, has been one of the mostly carved Buddhist texts in China. The first part of the article – the extant examples of the carved Lotus Sutra - lists the known examples in respect of the date, place, type, content and historical documents. The second part – the thoughts and technology of carving Buddhist sutras in China – argues that the practice of carving sutras shows the reverence for the Chinese characters and the trust on the durability of the stone. The carved texts or Buddha names on the cliffs in open air have often a strong symbolic meaning, and in many cases the characters can be regarded as the replacements of Buddha statues. The carved texts, either key paragraphs or Buddha names or the title of the sutras also represent the whole corresponding text. The function of the carved texts in certain spatial environment in the Northern Qi Dynasty (550-577) might have been related with the practice of meditation. In a similar manner, the Chapter of the Universal Gate or the Buddha names from Lotus Sutra or even its title have been carved, and they might assume the same power as the whole text. The third part – the religious thoughts and the characteristics of the carved Lotus Sutra – discusses: a. the contents of the carved Lotus Sutras ranging from the whole text to just the title; b. the thoughts of preserving all the Buddhist texts and the Lotus Sutra in seven places with other Buddhist texts; c. the cult of the bodhisattva Avalokiteśvara and the related carved text. Abstract by Zhao Zhou

titleInfo.title: Asai Endō sensei koki kinen ronbunshū: Nichiren kyōgaku no shomondai
浅井円道先生古稀記念論文集：日蓮教學の諸問題
